data.title: Investors Stunned as Amazon (AMZN) Halts 'Blue Jay' Robot Project Months After Launch
data.description:
Amazon (AMZN) has suddenly and without warning stopped development of its highly anticipated "Blue Jay" robot project months after launching it to much fanfare.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Project Blue Jay was a multi-armed robotic system that Amazon planned to develop and deploy at its same-day delivery warehouses. Executives at the technology giant touted the new robotic system as having the potential to revolutionize its warehouses with massive efficiency gains.

The project was launched last October and had been praised by investors as the latest advance in Amazon's efforts to speed up delivery times using robots. However, media reports have surfaced that Amazon quietly shut down the project at the end of January.

Amazon's Ongoing Struggles

Amazon employees who had been working full-time on the multi-armed robot have been reassigned to other robotics initiatives within the company, according to reports. Amazon has not commented publicly on the cancellation of the project or the reasons behind the decision.

However, some analysts note that the Blue Jay robotic system faced high costs and complexities that made it a controversial project inside Amazon. The tech giant is now planning to refocus its efforts on its "Orbital" system that aims to enhance same-day deliveries at smaller Amazon warehouses.

Is AMZN Stock a Buy?

AMZN stock has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 44 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 41 Buy and three Hold recommendations assigned in the last three months. The average AMZN price target of $282.14 implies 40.73% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: 'AI Disrupting AI': Top Investor Warns Palantir Stock (PLTR) Could Be in Trouble
data.description:
Palantir (NASDAQ:PLTR) has set demanding expectations for itself, and in recent quarters, it has managed to beat them. That's important because the upbeat tone from CEO Alex Karp isn't just bravado; it's backed by hard numbers.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The company's AI-driven offerings, especially its Artificial Intelligence Platform (AIP), have helped fuel robust adoption and growth, with Q4:25 revenue climbing about 70% year-over-year and U.S. commercial sales surging 137%, as enterprises increasingly deploy AI analytics and automation at scale. The momentum pushed full-year 2025 revenue up and led Palantir to issue bullish 2026 guidance, implying continued ~60% growth.

But the stronger the growth, the higher the bar becomes. With a market cap north of $300 billion – even after a 25% pullback this year – the stock still reflects expectations for sustained hypergrowth. At this valuation, even a modest deceleration could meaningfully alter the investment thesis.

That sensitivity to slowing momentum is exactly what concerns one top investor known as Stone Fox Capital. He argues that the very AI wave fueling Palantir's ascent could eventually work against it.

"The real risk is that investors see the threat of Claude Code allowing companies to build Palantir-type AI solutions internally," states Stone Fox, who is among the top 4% of stock pros covered by TipRanks.

In a somewhat ironic twist, the investor explains that this is "AI disrupting AI." Previously, Palantir's winning sales pitch to clients has been its ability to create bespoke AI solutions that improve various aspects of their business operations.

However, what if other AI options – such as Anthropic's recently-launched Claude Code – can replace Palantir's AIP? That would be a difficult pill to swallow.

"Suddenly, Palantir isn't the premier AI company and faces some legitimate threats of their technology being supplanted by the LLMs from Anthropic," cautions Stone Fox.

In addition, Stone Fox isn't totally convinced by Palantir's Rule of 40 score. Calling the 127% figure "so misleading," the investor points out that the company's margins don't take into account stock-based compensation.

As mentioned above, one of the biggest issues with Palantir for many investors, including Stone Fox, has been its "extreme valuations" over the course of its massive bull run. The investor adds that eventually PLTR's forward sales multiple will eventually decrease, just like "all of the greatest enterprise software stocks."

And that leaves PLTR's share price with just one way to go: downward.

"The stock is likely to continue re-rating as investors price in the risk that revenue growth doesn't occur in perpetuity due to AI threats," concludes Stone Fox, who rates PLTR a Strong Sell. (To watch Stone Fox Capital's track record, click here)

Wall Street, on the other hand, is feeling a bit better regarding which direction the winds are blowing. With 11 Buys, 6 Holds, and 2 Sells, PLTR enjoys a Moderate Buy consensus rating. Its 12-month average price target of $191.25 points to gains of ~44% in the year ahead. (See PLTR stock forecast)
data.author: Gabe Ross

data.title: "Let China Come In"; Ford Stock (NYSE:F) Slips as it Turns to Trump for Chinese EV Tech
data.description:
Legacy automaker Ford (F) may be about to play a dangerous game. It is turning to the Trump administration to get permission to bring in Chinese electric vehicle (EV) technology, a move which could stigmatize entire product lines. Ford investors certainly seem concerned, based on the fractional loss Ford shares suffered in Tuesday afternoon's trading.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Most Chinese electric vehicle solutions are, at best, difficult to get in the United States. Substantial tariffs are applied to such vehicles, and some are looking to put up complete bans on the platforms due to growing security risks. But some reports suggest that could change soon, as Ford is petitioning the president directly to let some of these products into the United States via partnership efforts.

This is actually similar to how the Chinese themselves allowed products from other countries to come in via joint development efforts. While products were not specifically banned, the Chinese tended to prefer joint venture operations, which would give Chinese companies a hand in the process. The idea that the United States might in turn use a similar approach is not out of line. However, one of Ford's biggest rivals, General Motors (GM), has made it clear it does not support any Chinese brands coming into the United States.

A Fight With Physics Itself

Meanwhile, reports suggest that Ford's own efforts to bring out electric vehicles are running into the most resilient problems of all: pure physics. Ford has made great strides of late, particularly in terms of the Universal EV Platform. But even these strides are starting to run into the wall that is the basic law of the universe, and so Ford is looking to make small improvements that add up over time.

Ford is putting out what it calls "bounties," or numbers assigned to major efficiency drivers like vehicle mass or aerodynamic drag. Because these factors directly impact key things like range and cost, these small changes can add up to big wins. The bounty system helps keep Ford's engineers focused on the things it can do right now, even as it looks for future developments like solid-state batteries to yield bigger changes later.

Is Ford Stock a Good Buy Right Now?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on F stock based on two Buys, 12 Holds and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 51.99% rally in its share price over the past year, the average F price target of $13.88 per share implies 1.56% downside risk.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Intel Looks for "One in Two" PCs to be AI-Driven; Intel Stock (NASDAQ:INTC) Slips Regardless
data.description:
The notion of an artificial intelligence (AI) PC has caught some imaginations, but has not made a lot of headway in the market. But chip stock Intel (INTC) looks for the AI PC phenomenon to take off in a very big way, eventually. Intel looks for one out of every two PCs to be AI PCs, and it looks for Panther Lake to lead the way therein. Investors were skeptical. They sent Intel shares down fractionally in Tuesday afternoon's trading.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Basically, Intel is convinced that its Panther Lake lineup is perfect for the AI PC market. So much so, in fact, that with Panther Lake chips in these devices, their adoption rate would soar, ultimately reaching right around half of the overall PC market. Most AI PCs have suffered from a lack of overall computing power, but the Panther Lake lineup looks to deliver the necessary power, and thus improve adoption rates.

Intel Japan CEO Makoto Ohno noted, "It is also predicted that by 2026, this will exceed half, with one in two computers being an AI PC. However, given the current situation, reasons for choosing an AI PC include its high performance and the long battery life provided by the use of an NPU. In other words, it is important to reflect on the fact that people are not currently purchasing an AI PC in order to use its AI-related functions."

Xe Next

That is not all that is up Intel's collective sleeve, though. Intel is currently working on the Xe Next lineup, which is the next generation of graphics processing unit (GPU) property. It has no official name as yet, and there is not much information surrounding what it can do. But considering that it is set to be geared toward data centers, reports note, it suggests that Xe Next will be a significant power in the market.

But Xe Next will come after many of Intel's current products, like the Jaguar Shores lineup. Reports suggest that Jaguar Shores will be ready in the middle of the year, with a potential release either late this year, or early in 2027. And Intel is also working on further Arc releases, the Celestial and the Druid. Getting these into the field should augment Intel's performance at a time when it sorely needs new market share.

Is Intel a Buy, Hold or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on INTC stock based on eight Buys, 21 Holds and four Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 70.83% rally in its share price over the past year, the average INTC price target of $48.21 per share implies 3.57% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Is a Tesla Robotaxi Actually More Likely to Crash than a Human?
data.description:
Tesla's (TSLA) robotaxi fleet in Austin, Texas, has reportedly been involved in 14 crashes since June 2025. Importantly, five of those incidents happened in December and January, which has raised new concerns about the safety of the fully autonomous vehicles. Notably, Electrek reported that Tesla's robotaxis had driven roughly 800,000 miles by mid-January, based on information from the company's fourth-quarter earnings report. With 14 crashes recorded, that works out to about one crash every 57,000 miles.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

By comparison, NHTSA data from 2022 shows that human drivers experience one minor or major crash approximately every 500,000 miles. As a result, this suggests that Tesla's robotaxis could be about eight times more likely to crash than a human driver. In addition, the report noted that Tesla provides limited public details about these incidents.

For instance, Tesla often redacts information and labels certain crash data as "confidential business information," which differs from how some competitors disclose reports. Moreover, a July crash was later updated to include an injury, even though the original filing described it as property damage only. Because of this, some observers argue that the available data may not provide a complete picture of the situation.

What Is the Prediction for TSLA Stock?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on TSLA stock based on 12 Buys, 11 Holds, and seven Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average TSLA price target of $396.80 per share implies 3.8% downside risk.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: U.S. Stock Futures Fall as AI Disruption Fears Continue to Drag Down Tech Stocks
data.description:
U.S. stock futures fell on Tuesday morning, kicking off a holiday-shortened trading week in the red amid continued fears of artificial intelligence (AI) disrupting software and other businesses. Meanwhile, traders await key employment figures, FOMC minutes, and delayed economic reports. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were down 0.79%, 0.38%, and 0.15%, respectively, at 8:36 a.m. EST on February 17.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Last week, all three major indexes closed lower. The S&P 500 and Dow Jones each dropped more than 1%, while the Nasdaq Composite fell over 2%, marking its fifth straight week of losses. Ongoing concerns about AI disrupting software, real estate, and financial services weighed on investor sentiment.

Turning to trending tickers, shares of health tech giant Masimo (MASI) rallied more than 34% in pre-market trading following reports that the company was nearing a deal with Danaher (DHR) to be acquired for about $10 billion in cash. Danaher stock declined about 6% on the news. Meanwhile, Norwegian Cruise Line (NCLH) stock jumped 6% following a Wall Street Journal report that Elliott Investment Management has built a more than 10% stake in the company.

Also, Paramount Skydance (PSKY) stock rose about 3% after Netflix (NFLX) granted Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) a seven-day waiver to hold deal talks with Paramount. WBD stock gained 2% on the news.

data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: Boeing Stock (NYSE:BA) Notches Up With Another Big Order
data.description:
Aerospace giant Boeing (BA) may not have the easiest time filling all its orders, but it sure has an easier time getting them. In fact, Boeing recently landed another major order, with the flag carrier of Kazakhstan, Air Astana, finalizing an order for as many as 15 Dreamliners. The news was good enough for investors, though, who have Boeing shares a fractional boost in Tuesday afternoon's trading.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Air Astana was looking to modernize its fleet of Boeing widebody jets, and turned to Boeing once more for its Dreamliner jets. This is not so much a new order, so much as it is a finalized previous order, however. Air Astana originally looked to place that order back in November, but has now finalized the order.

The Air Astana order is actually exciting for another reason. This purchase represents the single largest order of aircraft in Air Astana's history, reports note. It goes to demonstrate the impact that Boeing has on the market, especially since a European airline would be more likely, by some measurements, to turn to Airbus (EADSY) anyway.

Oh, About Airbus….

But then, something particularly unexpected happened that dampened spirits at Boeing. Airbus actually augmented its partnership with Palantir Technologies (PLTR), setting up a new multi-year agreement between the two. Palantir and Airbus actually got together to develop Skywise, an open-data platform that brings together in-flight engineering and operational data.

Skywise is used by over 50,000 different users, and will help to improve supply chain operations in the aircraft market. It also puts together several large language models (LLMs) to deliver AI-enhanced functions, making it an eye-catching opportunity in the broader market.

Is Boeing a Good Stock to Buy Right Now?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on BA stock based on 14 Buys, one Hold and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 31.35% rally in its share price over the past year, the average BA price target of $269.50 per share implies 10.28% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: How High Can Alphabet (GOOG) and Meta Platforms (META) Go? Street-High Price Targets Tell a Bullish Tale
data.description:
This earnings season has been a curious one for the market's tech giants. While the hyperscalers have shown strength by mostly delivering blowout readouts, investors have reacted with caution rather than euphoria. The hesitation centers on the amount of cash these companies plan to spend to realize their AI ambitions.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The four major hyperscalers – Amazon (AMZN), Microsoft (MSFT), Meta (META), and Alphabet (GOOG) – are now expected to lift capex 60%-70% above the record levels seen in 2025, with combined outlays approaching $700 billion as they invest heavily in top-end chips, construct massive new data centers, and acquire the networking infrastructure required to link everything together. Hitting that level of spending will likely result in a significant reduction in free cash flow.

So, Wall Street is wondering whether all that spending will payoff. Will companies like Alphabet and Meta be able to ultimately justify the massive investment?

Some Street analysts are confident they will be able to do so, having recently assigned Street-high price targets for these names, but does the rest of the Street agree that this is the case? We've opened the TipRanks database to find out.

Alphabet

The market appears to be jittery about AI right now, and that is a sentiment Alphabet investors will be familiar with. When ChatGPT burst into widespread public use in late 2022, many investors interpreted it as a direct threat to Alphabet, Google's parent company. The logic behind that concern was not that outlandish: If people started using conversational AI instead of traditional search, it could eat into the ad revenue that Google relies on. Given how central search is to Google's revenue model and market dominance, any shift in how people search online introduces the real risk of disruption.

That line of thought, however, overlooked the foundation Alphabet had already built. The company had spent years researching AI and Google was quick to react, integrating generative AI directly into Search, Cloud, and its wider ecosystem of products.

And as investors realized that Google's capabilities in the field were very strong, they reacted accordingly – by pushing shares higher. Ultimately, GOOGL stock saw out 2025 as the biggest winner among the Mag 7, with the market seeing it as possibly best-positioned to make the most of the AI opportunity over the long term.

The company's recent Q4 print only gave further credence to that idea. Revenue reached $113.83 billion, representing 18% year-over-year growth and surpassing the $111.4 billion estimate, while EPS of $2.82 exceeded the Street's $2.64 forecast. Google Cloud was a standout, with revenue climbing 47.8% YoY to $17.66 billion. But investors reacted negatively to the print for a simple reason: the company guided to 2026 capital expenditures of $175 billion to $185 billion to fund ongoing AI-related expansion, roughly double the previous year's outlay and well above the $119.5 billion analysts had been anticipating.

Should investors be worried, though? Not according to Canaccord analyst Maria Ripps, who points to the positive developments taking place against the backdrop of huge spending. 

"The pace of Cloud deals is showing no signs of slowing, with Google exiting the year with double the new customer velocity compared to Q1, and existing customers are outpacing their initial commitments by over 30%. To capitalize on this momentum, Google guided to a step-change increase in FY26 CapEx to $175-185B, roughly double the prior year's investment. In our view, a commitment of this magnitude reflects Google's strong conviction in the scale of the AI opportunity, though the size of the investment appears to be tempering the stock's… reaction. That said, accelerating Search momentum, a rapidly expanding Cloud backlog, and tangible Gemini adoption suggest that Google is successfully leading the transition to an AI-native economy, and this print further reinforces our view that the stock should be a core holding in large-cap tech portfolios."

Accordingly, Ripps rates Alphabet shares a Buy while her Street-high $415 price target offers 12-month upside of 37%. (To watch Ripps' track record, click here)

She's hardly alone in her optimism. The broader analyst community stacks up 24 additional Buys against 7 Holds, giving the stock a Strong Buy consensus rating. Meanwhile, the average price target of $379.47 points to a 25% gain over the coming year. (See GOOGL stock forecast)
data.author: Marty Shtrubel

data.title: Anthropic Launches Another AI Model as the Nightmare Continues for Software Stocks
data.description:
AI giant Anthropic just introduced Claude Sonnet 4.6, its second major AI model release in less than two weeks. Notably, the new version improves performance in areas such as computer use, coding, design work, general knowledge tasks, and handling large datasets. As a result, Claude Sonnet 4.6 will now become the default model for both free users and paid Pro subscribers inside the Claude chatbot and the Claude Cowork productivity tool.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Importantly, the launch shows just how intense the pace of innovation in the AI sector is, where companies are investing heavily to stay competitive. In fact, this release comes just 12 days after Anthropic rolled out Claude Opus 4.6. According to the company, tasks that previously required its more powerful Opus model can now be handled by Sonnet 4.6, such as real-world office and coding assignments.

In particular, Anthropic emphasized that the model delivers much stronger, more consistent coding performance and follows instructions better. However, these improvements may add to investor concerns that AI could disrupt traditional software businesses, as quick AI advancements have already contributed to a steep selloff in software stocks. More specifically, the iShares Expanded Tech-Software Sector ETF (IGV) has fallen by more than 20% on a year-to-date basis.

Is IGV Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on IGV stock based on 91 Buys, 22 Holds, and zero Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average IGV price target of $128.86 per share implies 59.1% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: ServiceNow (NOW) CEO Buys $3 Million of Company Stock as Software Implodes
data.description:
ServiceNow (NOW) CEO William "Bill" McDermott has bought $3 million of company stock on the open market as the software sector implodes and share prices crash.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The big purchase by the CEO comes with NOW stock down 30% this year and down 50% from its peak last summer of $211. In a statement announcing the stock purchase, McDermott said, "I'm investing $3M of my own money because I have absolute conviction in the value we're building… There is no better entry point I can imagine."

In a securities filing with regulators, ServiceNow also said that other executives at the software company are canceling planned share sales and plan to continue holding onto NOW stock. "This is a once-in-a-generation moment with ServiceNow at the forefront of innovation as the AI control tower for business reinvention," said McDermott.

Software's AI Problem

News that ServiceNow's CEO is buying millions of dollars worth of company stock wasn't enough to give the shares a boost. In afternoon trading on Feb. 17, NOW stock was down 1.30% after McDermott publicly announced his share purchase.
data.author: Joel Baglole
